quoteFirst: Howe, by carrying sail, was thus able to choose his position prior to bearing down. Having reached it and formed his line, the French being now hove-to to await the attack, the British fleet was also hove-to, and the crews went to breakfast. At
quoteLast: it again filled-away by signal, and stood slowly down for the enemy (June 1, Fig. 1). The intention being to attack along the whole line, ship to ship, advantage was taken of this measured approach to change the place of the three-decked ships in the order, so that they should be opposed to the heaviest of the French.
title: The Influence of Sea Power upon the French Revolution and Empire 1793-1812, vol 1
author: Mahan, A. T. (Alfred Thayer), 1840-1914
